name: unreal-engine
description: Drive Unreal Editor through Epic's native Unreal MCP server with inspected, serialized, and verified operations.

companion.title: Enable Unreal MCP in Unreal Engine 5.8
companion.summary: Use Epic's built-in, experimental local MCP server; it is not the legacy community Python bridge.
steps: Open the UE 5.8 project, then select Edit → Plugins, search for 'Unreal MCP', enable it, and restart the editor.
Open Edit → Editor Preferences → General → Model Context Protocol and enable Auto Start Server. Keep the default local endpoint unless you intentionally change it: http://127.0.0.1:8000/mcp.

In the editor console, run ModelContextProtocol.GenerateClientConfig All (or ClaudeCode, Codex, Cursor, VSCode, or Gemini). This generates the client configuration from the editor's actual port and path.
Enable the AllToolsets plugin, then reconnect the agent so Unreal MCP can advertise useful toolsets.
prerequisites: Unreal Engine 5.8; Unreal MCP is an Experimental built-in plugin.
Keep the server on loopback. It has no authentication and is not safe to expose remotely.
